content.full_text:
Fern PolrrrcAt- PnecrrcBs CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street r Suire 620 o Sacramento. CA 95g1.1_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax t9l6) 322-0886
January 15,2008
Bobbie Velasquez
159 Lindero
Lindsay, CA93247
Via facsimile and
U.S. mail
Dear Ms. Velasquez:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding your duties as a member
of the lindsay Hospital District Board as they relate to the conilicrof-interesr provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act").1
Please note that our advice is based solely on provisions of the Act. We therefore
offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflicrof-interest laws such as
common law conflict-of-interest or Government Code Section 1090. In addition, our
advice is based solely on the facts presented in your request; the Commission does not act
as a finder of fact when it provides advice. (ln re ogleiby (1975) I Fppc ops. 71.)
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. .4-08-206
QUESTTON
May you participate in Board decisions involving the City of Lindsay (..City,,) or
the Lindsay School District ("District',)?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Under the facts you have provided you do not have a conf-lict-of-interest in
participating in governmental decisions involving the City or the District because your
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in covernment Code Sections g tOff) through gl0l.1. AII
statutory ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through lnggT of Title 2 of the Calilornia
Code of Regulations. All re,qulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Calitirrnia code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. 4-08-206
Page No. 2
husband's stipend from the city and your salary from the school district both fall under
the Act's "govemment salary exception."
FACTS
You were elected to the Lindsay Hospital District Board (.,Board',) on
November 4,2008. Since then, legal counsel for the Board has raised questions
regarding a potential conflict-of-interest in your serving on the Board.
Your husband is currently Mayor Pro Tem on the Lindsay City Council. He
receives a $50-a-month stipend from the city for serving on the city council. During our
telephone conversation of December 18,2008, you stat-d that the board is cunently
funding a city project called the Wellness Center. The Board has given about $900;000
to this project over several years. However, neither you nor your husband has any
financial interest in the project.
You are employed by the Lindsay Unified School District as an administrative
assistant to the Superintendent. Each year, the Board funds a portion of the school
district nurse's salary. You are not related to the school nurse, nor do you benefit
financially from her position as school nurse.
You wish to know if either your husband's position as Mayor pro Tem on the city
council or your position as an administrative assistant:at the school district presents a
conflict-of-interest in your serving on the Lindsay Hospital District Board.
A1\[ALYSIS
step 1. Are you a "public official,, within the meaning of section g7100?
Section 82048 defines a public official as "every member, officer, employee or
consultant of a state or local government agency." Because you are a membei of the
local hospital district board, a local government agency, you are a public official.
step 2. will you be making, participating in making or influencing a
governmental decision?
A public official "makes a govemmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his-or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contrictual
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Regulation 1g702.1.)
A public official "participates in a governmental decision" when, acting within
the authority of his or her position and without significant substantive interveriing review,


File No. ,4-08-206
Page No. 3
the official negotiates, advises, or makes recommendations to the decisionmaker
regarding the governmental decision. (Regulation Ig7 OZ.Z.)
A public official is attempting to use his or her official position to influence a
governmental decision if, for the purpose of influencing the decision, the official contacts
or appears before or otherwise attempts to influence, any member, officer, employee, or
consultant of his or her agency. (Regulation 18702.3(a).) With regard to governmental
decisions which are within or before an agency other than the official's agJncy, the
official is attempting to use his official position to influence the decision-ifl for the
purpose of influencing the decision, the official acts or purports to act on behalf of, or as
the representative of, his or her agency to any member, officer, employee or consultant of
an agency. (Regulation I 8702.3(b).)
If you make, participate in making, or influence decisions involving the City or
the District that come before Board, you will be making govemmental decisions.
Step 3. What are your economic interests?
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from certain enumerated economic interests. These economic interests are described in
Section 87103 and Regulations 18703-18703.5, inclusive:
o A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
has a direct or indirect investment of g2,000 or more. (Section g7103(a);
Regulation I 8703. l(a).)
o A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds anyposition of
management. (Section 87 103(d); Regulation 19703. l(b).)
o A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section gTiOf G); Regulation
18703.2.)
o An official has an economic interest in any source of income, including promised
income, totaling $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. [Section
87103(c); Regulation I 8703.3.)
o I public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if
the gifts total $420 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section
87 103(e); Regulation t8703.4.)
o A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses, income,
assets, or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediate family. This is


File No. 4-08-206
Page No. 4
commonly referred to as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103;
Regulation 18703.5.)
Economic Interests Identified
Source of Income: Your question focuses on potential conflicts arising from your
husband's employment with the city, and your employment with the school district. As
noted above, a public official has an economic interest in his or her own income (Section
87103; Regulation 18703.5) and in the source of that income. (Section 87103(c);
Regulation 18703.3.) Income includes any community property interest in the income of
a spouse. (Section 82030.) Therefore, you have an economic interest in your husband's
income, and his source of income, the City. You also have an economic interest in your
own income, and the source of that income, the District.
Government Salary Exception: Ordinarily, a public official has an economic
interest in his or her income, as well as the income ol t ir or her immediate family.
(Section 87103; Regulation 13703.5.) However, where the employer is a government
agency there is typically no economic interest because of the "gtu"*."nt salary
exception." (Section 82030(b)(2), copy enclosed.) The Act's definition of ,.income,,
expressly excludes "salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from
a...local ... governmentagency ...." (lbid.) Theexceptionulro.ou".remployment-
related benefits such as pensions. (ln re Moore (1977): ippc ops. 33.)
Because both the City and the District are local government agencies under
Section 82041, the stipend paid to your husband, and the salary paid 6 you, are not
considered "income" under S ection 82030(b)(2).
Therefore, under the "governmental salary" exception, you do not have an
economic interest in either the city or the school district as a source of income.
Personal Finances: Every public official has an economic interest in his or her
personal finances and those of hii or her immediate family. (Section 87103; Regulation
18703.5.) A govemmental decision will have an affect on this economic interest if the
decision will result in your or your immediate family's personal expenses, income, assets
or liabilities, other than the financial affect on your reaiproperty inierest or business,
increasing or decreasing. (lbid.)
You have not provided any other facts indicating your participation in any
government decision involving the city or the school district would off".t your personal
finances in any way. Therefore we do not further analyze your personal finances as a
potential conflict of interest.


File No. 4-08-206
Page No. 5
Absent an economic interest in the decision, a conflict of interest does not exist.
Therefore, under the facts you provided, you do not have a conflict-of-interest in
participating in Board decisions involving either the City or the District.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (gt6) j22-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ERjgl
Enclosure
